task_summary: Pack office items into an open carton on a desk. The demonstration places a picture frame, a spiral notebook and a mug into the carton; it does not close the carton and leaves other desk items unpacked.

evidence.note: open cardboard box at the back of the desk, contains white packing paper
silver/gold picture frame holding a photo of two people; first item packed
cream spiral-bound notebook; second item packed
light blue ceramic mug sitting on the desk; packed last

initial_state.description: The carton is open and empty apart from white packing paper.

evidence.note: box open and empty at the start

initial_state.description: The picture frame, notebook and mug are on the desk, not yet packed.

steps.description: Pick up the picture frame and place it into the carton.

evidence.note: frame lifted ~2.5s, lying inside the carton by ~3.7s

steps.description: Place the spiral notebook into the carton on top of the frame.
Pick up the mug and lower it into the carton.

evidence.note: mug picked up ~7.0s and lowered into the carton at the end of the clip

final_goals.description: The picture frame, notebook and mug are inside the carton.

evidence.note: all three items in the carton by the end of the clip

evidence.quote: Pack the visible office items into the carton and close it.
evidence.note: closing is stated in the description but is NOT shown in the video; the clip ends with the carton open

ordering.rationale: The frame was placed before the notebook, but nothing indicates the order is required; the items are independent.
The notebook was placed before the mug, but the order appears incidental.

unknowns.question: Should the workflow pack only the picture frame, notebook and mug demonstrated, or all visible office supplies (e.g. the markers, highlighter pack and folders left on the desk)?
unknowns.why_it_matters: It defines the scope of the task - which objects the robot must pack and when the task is complete.
